title: Magic 8.3.105 integration issues (historical)
observed_symptom: Magic 8.3.105 (Ubuntu apt package) failed in GLI-FLOW batch mode due to wrapper script, tech file version mismatch, and CAD_ROOT env var conflict.
environment: Ubuntu apt package magic 8.3.105+ds.1-1.1, GLI-FLOW prior to integration fixes
root_cause: Environment-specific packaging issues, not a Magic version defect. Three distinct causes: (1) /usr/bin/magic is a Tcl/Tk Wish wrapper that fails in -dnull batch mode; (2) sky130A.tech required magic-8.3.411 but apt provides 8.3.105; (3) CAD_ROOT env var in safe_env() interfered with Magic resource lookup.
evidence: MAGIC_ROOT_CAUSE.md documents all three causes. FIRST_PASS_REPORT.md documents fixes applied. Functional validation (2026-06-08) confirms Magic 8.3.105 passes: TCL startup, batch execution via magicdnull, DRC check, DRC report generation, exit codes.
resolution: Three fixes applied: (1) Use magicdnull -nowrapper -d NULL for batch mode instead of /usr/bin/magic wrapper; (2) Patched sky130A.tech to require magic-8.3.0; (3) Removed CAD_ROOT from safe_env(). Blacklist replaced with historical risk warning + functional validation.
historical_context: Version 8.3.105 was blacklisted in KNOWN_BROKEN_VERSIONS commit 9af9e43. The blacklist was replaced with HISTORICAL_RISK_VERSIONS during audit (2026-06-08) after functional validation proved the version is not inherently broken. Version-only rejection replaced with version warning + functional validation pipeline.
recommendation: Keep historical risk flag for informational purposes. Do not fail based on version alone. Rely on validate_magic_functionality() for pass/fail decision.
remediation: Ensure magicdnull is used for batch mode (not /usr/bin/magic wrapper). If tech file version mismatch occurs, patch sky130A.tech requires line to magic-8.3.0.
